title: family_aware_permutation_design_v1 — null-model permutation blocks per (focal_inversion × parent)
description: Typed payload for the interchromosomal page's family-aware permutation null. One row per (focal_inversion_id, parent_id). The consumer picks n_permutations at run time (page UI exposes 1k / 10k / 100k) and shuffles karyotype labels within permutation_block to preserve sib-correlation. Rows with unknown karyotype enum or missing identifiers are dropped at normalize time.

focal_inversion_id.description: Which inversion's null model this row applies to. A separate design exists per inversion.

karyotype.description: Karyotype at focal_inversion_id. Unknown values drop the row.

permutation_block.description: Equivalence-class id for shuffling. Typically equals family_id; producer policy may coarsen or refine.

hub_id.description: Optional: ngsPedigree hub id (intermediate grouping). Diagnostics only; not used in the permutation.

n_offspring.description: Optional: count of offspring this parent contributes. Used by the consumer if the null model weights by offspring count.

n_assignments.description: Total row count after coercion + identifier drop.

n_permutation_blocks.description: Distinct permutation_block values across all focal inversions.

karyotype_counts.description: Aggregate karyotype tally across all focal inversions; surfaces het / non-het balance.

n_singleton_blocks.description: Permutation blocks containing exactly one parent (the permutation is trivially identity for these). High N means degenerate design; consumer should widen blocks.
